figures.caption: Fig. 1. Scenario with an unknown number of objects L(t), each with their own kinematic parameters Φ̃ℓ(t) being observed by L NT ×NR MIMO radars, resulting in complex amplitudes α̃ℓ,l(τ) per object and radar.

Fig. 3. Tracks of the considered scenarios. (a) The random crossing tracks scenario observed by four radars located on a circle at p1 = [ −100 160 ]T , p2 = [ 10 50 ]T , p3 = [ −100 −60 ]T , and p4 = [ −210 50 ]T . (b) Paralell track scenario with the radars placed in the same locations as shown in (a). (c) Sensor handover scenario with radars placed at pi =[ −100 + (i− 1)110 0 ]T . The dashed lines denote the mean distance resulting in the return signal haveing a SNR of -20 dB.

Fig. 2. Bayesian network representation of the multi-object detection and tracking problem. Each object k has state Φk,n, existence ξk,n, and reflectivity αk,n,l. Shaded nodes denote the measured data Zn and clear nodes represent unknown random variables.
Fig. 4. The OSPA error for the random crossing tracks
